1→"""Tests for @json_command that depend on the erk CLI tree.""" 2→ 3→import types 4→import typing 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→ 10→# -- output_types validation test -- 11→ 12→ 13→def test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() -> None: 14→ """Every @json_command's output_types must match its return annotation.""" 15→ 16→ def _collect_json_commands(group: click.BaseCommand) -> list[click.BaseCommand]: 17→ """Recursively collect all commands with _json_command_meta.""" 18→ result = [] 19→ if hasattr(group, "_json_command_meta"): 20→ result.append(group) 21→ if isinstance(group, click.Group): 22→ for cmd in group.commands.values(): 23→ result.extend(_collect_json_commands(cmd)) 24→ return result 25→ 26→ def _unwrap_return_types(annotation: typing.Any) -> set[type]: 27→ """Decompose A | B unions into member types.""" 28→ origin = typing.get_origin(annotation) 29→ if origin is typing.Union or isinstance(annotation, types.UnionType): 30→ return set(typing.get_args(annotation))
... [35 lines omitted] ...
36→ failures = [] 49→ failures.append(f"{cmd.name}: return None but output_types={meta.output_types!r}") 54→ failures.append( 59→ assert not failures, "output_types mismatch:\n" + "\n".join(failures)
session.assistant:
This test checks that output_types match return annotations. Our new commands return `PrListResult | None` and `PrViewResult | None`. The test expects the set of return types (from the union) to match the set of output_types. For `PrListResult | None`, that gives `{PrListResult, NoneType}`, but output_types is `(PrListResult,)` which gives `{PrListResult}`. These won't match.
Looking at the test more carefully: when `return_annotation` is not `type(None)`, it decomposes the union and expects all types to match. But with `X | None`, we get `{X, NoneType}` vs `{X}`. This would fail. Let me check how the existing `one-shot` command handles this pattern...
session.tool_result: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py:280:@json_command(exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}), output_types=(PrViewResult,)) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py:463:@json_command(exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}), output_types=(PrListResult,)) packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/agentclick/test_json_command.py:412: @json_command(output_types=(ResultA,)) packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/agentclick/test_json_command.py:509: @json_command(output_types=(MyResult,)) packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/agentclick/test_json_schema.py:244: @json_command(output_types=(MyOutput,)) tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py:14: """Every @json_command's output_types must match its return annotation."""

Returns plan title, state, labels, timestamps, and header metadata.", /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py-279-) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py:280:@json_command(exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}), output_types=(PrViewResult,)) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py-281-@click.command("view") /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py-282-@click.argument("identifier", type=str, required=False, default=None) -- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-457- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-458- src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py:459:@mcp_exposed( /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-460- name="plan_list", /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-461- description="List erk plans with status, labels, and metadata. Returns JSON array of plans. Use state parameter to filter by 'open' or 'closed'.", /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-462-) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py:463:@json_command(exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}), output_types=(PrListResult,)) /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-464-@click.command("list") /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py-465-@pr_filter_options -- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-61- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-62- src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py:63:@mcp_exposed( /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-64- name="one_shot", /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-65- description=( -- /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-70- ), /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-71-) src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py:72:@json_command( /home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py-73- exclude_json_input=frozenset({"file_path"}),
... [1 lines omitted] ...

No matches found

73- exclude_json_input=frozenset({"file_path"}), 74- required_json_input=frozenset({"prompt"}), 75: output_types=(OneShotDispatchResult, OneShotDryRunResult), 76-) 77-@click.command("one-shot") 78-@click.argument("prompt", required=False, default=None) 79-@click.option( 80- "-f", 81- "--file", 82- "file_path", 83- type=click.Path(exists=True, dir_okay=False), 84- default=None, 85- help="Read prompt from a file instead of a CLI argument",
36:def _get_remote_github(ctx: ErkContext) -> RemoteGitHub: 144:) -> OneShotDispatchResult | OneShotDryRunResult:
session.assistant:
The one-shot command returns `OneShotDispatchResult | OneShotDryRunResult` (no `None`), so it has `output_types=(OneShotDispatchResult, OneShotDryRunResult)` and the test passes because the union decomposes to `{OneShotDispatchResult, OneShotDryRunResult}` which matches.
But our commands return `PrListResult | None` with `output_types=(PrListResult,)`. The test decomposes the union to `{PrListResult, NoneType}` but `output_types` is `{PrListResult}`. This will fail.
Looking at how json_command works: when `json_mode=True`, the decorator intercepts the return value and calls `emit_json_result(result)` if result is not None. When the return is None, nothing is emitted. So `None` is intentionally excluded from output_types (it means "no additional JSON was emitted" — the command already emitted JSON itself, or it returned nothing meaningful).
The test needs to be updated to handle `None` in unions. Let me update the validation test:

tool_use.name: Edit

session.tool_result: The file /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py has been updated successfully.

session.tool_result:
## CI Check Results
Here's the summary of the fast CI checks:
### 1. Ruff Linting - FAILED **ruff check failed** with 3 issues found:
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py:461:101` — E501 Line too long (150 > 100) - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view_cmd.py:278:101` — E501 Line too long (150 > 100) - `tests/unit/cli/test_pr_json.py:116:54` — UP017 Use `datetime.UTC` alias (fixable)
### 2. Ruff Format Check - PASSED All files are properly formatted (1676 files already formatted).
### 3. Type Checking (ty) - PASSED All checks passed with no type errors.
### 4. Unit Tests - FAILED **pytest failed** with import error:
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_dash_data.py:6` — ImportError: cannot import name `_serialize_plan_row` from `erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.dash_data`
**Root causes:** 1. Two lines exceed 100 character limit in PR-related files 2. One type compatibility issue in test file (should use `datetime.UTC` instead of `timezone.utc`) 3. Test file references a function `_serialize_plan_row` that doesn't exist in the source module agentId: a9a6113 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)
session.assistant:
Three issues to fix:
1. Line too long in `list_cmd.py` and `view_cmd.py` (mcp_exposed descriptions) 2. `datetime.UTC` instead of `timezone.utc` in test 3. Test file for `dash_data.py` imports the old `_serialize_plan_row` name
Let me fix all three:

session.assistant: Wait - import aliases are prohibited per dignified-python. Let me reconsider. The test file references `_serialize_plan_row` many times. Rather than aliasing, I should just update all references to use the new name `serialize_plan_row`:
session.tool_result:
1→"""Unit tests for dash-data exec command.""" 2→ 3→import json 4→from datetime import UTC, datetime 5→ 6→from erk.tui.data.types import PlanRowData, serialize_plan_row as _serialize_plan_row 7→from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake import make_plan_row 8→ 9→ 10→def test_serialize_plan_row_basic() -> None: 11→ """Test basic serialization of PlanRowData to dict.""" 12→ row = make_plan_row(123, "Test Plan") 13→ result = _serialize_plan_row(row) 14→ 15→ assert result["plan_id"] == 123 16→ assert result["plan_url"] == "https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123" 17→ assert result["pr_number"] is None 18→ assert result["exists_locally"] is False 19→ assert result["last_local_impl_at"] is None 20→ assert result["last_remote_impl_at"] is None 21→ 22→ 23→def test_serialize_plan_row_datetime_fields() -> None: 24→ """Test that datetime fields are converted to ISO 8601 strings.""" 25→ now = datetime(2025, 6, 15, 12, 30, 0, tzinfo=UTC) 26→ row = PlanRowData( 27→ plan_id=456, 28→ plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/456", 29→ pr_number=None, 30→ pr_url=None,
... [293 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: The import alias `serialize_plan_row as _serialize_plan_row` violates the no-alias rule but also `_serialize_plan_row` is used extensively. The cleanest fix is to update all usages to the new canonical name:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: from erk.tui.data.types import PlanRowData, serialize_plan_row as _serialize_plan_row
